summary: Gate evidence is sufficient: the Visual QA fallback is explicit, current-head bound, residual-authoritative, and covered by focused regression tests without creating an implicit/no-op gate.
inspection_summary: Inspected the gate review request, PR evidence artifacts, the five changed story/spec/architecture/source/test files, and reran the focused Visual QA test command; the evidence supports a pass for gate_evidence.

.vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-visual-evidence-gate-ux/gate-dag.json: spec_status=present, path_surface_matrix_status=passed, artifact_consistency_status=passed, gate:visual_qa=ready_for_review, and current artifacts are strict_current rather than stale reuse
src/pr-manager.js:3942-3944 binds verification to git, reads residual .vibepro/qa evidence first, and only then uses buildVisualQaEvidenceFromVerification as fallback
src/pr-manager.js:4499-4540 accepts verification fallback only for current passing commands classified as both visual_qa and screenshot

judgment_delta: Initial concern: current visual evidence might weaken Visual QA into a generic/no-op fallback -> final judgment: fallback is bounded because src/pr-manager.js requires current pass plus visual_qa and screenshot markers and tests reject generic npm test evidence

Initial concern: residual .vibepro/qa artifacts might be bypassed -> final judgment: residual evidence remains authoritative because src/pr-manager.js reads artifactVisualQaEvidence first and VQG-S-4 proves above-threshold residuals keep gate:visual_qa needs_review

Initial concern: spec gate could be implicit -> final judgment: gate-dag spec node is present with docs/specs/story-vibepro-visual-evidence-gate-ux.md and traceability maps all five ACs
